title: 3.2.3 KMP Algorithm
subtitle: Prefix table and optimized matching, reserved for visualization/code/practice

content.introduction: KMP improves exact matching by using prefix information to avoid redundant comparisons after mismatches.

sections.title: 3.2.3.1 Key Ideas and Model
sections.content: Define longest proper prefix-suffix (LPS/next) and explain how it preserves matched information across mismatches.
examples: Explain fallback from mismatch using LPS value
Show why re-checking matched prefix is avoided

sections.title: 3.2.3.2 Representation and Workflow
sections.content: Describe two phases: building LPS in O(m), then scanning text in O(n) by moving pattern index via LPS instead of full reset.
examples: Report all match indices by continuing after a match
Handle mismatch when j is zero vs non-zero

sections.title: 3.2.3.3 Complexity and Practical Guidance
sections.content: Total complexity is O(n + m) with O(m) extra space. Discuss why KMP is stable on repetitive inputs where BF degrades.
examples: Compare KMP and BF under repetitive text
Estimate memory overhead of the LPS array
Use KMP for predictable performance in search-heavy services

keyTakeaways: Apply KMP to large-scale exact search tasks
Compare preprocessing cost against runtime gains
